versions.rationale: A Proof of Concept Study to Investigate the Feasibility of Targeted Release of Doxorubicin from Lyso-thermosensitive Liposomal (LTSL) Doxorubicin (ThermoDox®) Using Focused Ultrasound in Patients with Primary or Secondary Liver Tumours

population.name: Main Study Population
population.label: cancer patients

cohorts.name: Part I
cohorts.label: Part I will identify optimal FUS exposure parameters for a range of patient BMIs and tumour locations within the liver using real time thermometry data from an implanted thermometry device
cohorts.description: After at least 5 participants have had the intervention using real-time thermometry, data will be reviewed by the Trial Management Group (TMG) to confirm readiness to proceed without real-time thermometry (Part II).

cohorts.name: Part II
cohorts.label: Part II, which does not require implantation of a thermometry device
cohorts.description: Part II, which does not require implantation of a thermometry device, is designed to reflect how the therapy would be implemented in clinical practice. All evaluable participants from both Part I and Part II will be included in the endpoint analysis.

population.description: Phase 1 prospective non-randomised open label single site study to determine whether targeted release of doxorubicin from ThermoDox® ('drug') using mild hyperthermia generated non-invasively by FUS is possible in cancer patients. Single liver tumour previously selected for targeted drug delivery.

arms.name: ThermoDox® and FUS
arms.description: All participants will receive ThermoDox® and FUS.

studyDesigns.name: TARDOX Interventional Study Design
studyDesigns.label: Phase 1 prospective non-randomised open label single site study

epochs.name: TREATMENT
epochs.label: Preparation, Intervention
epochs.description: Participant attends (may be admitted) the day PET-CT and Pre-medication. Chart with Participant under General Anaesthetic. Single liver tumour previously selected for targeted drug delivery is identified by Ultrasound. Coaxial needle inserted and used to take first biopsy of tumour and first ('pre-treatment') blood test taken. Tumour exposed to focused ultrasound to induce regional hyperthermia monitored with thermometer, which is inserted through co-axial needle. Once tumour cools, chemotherapy agent (drug) is given through a peripheral vein over 30mins. Tumour has second biopsy taken and second ('unreleased') blood test is taken. Tumour again exposed to focused ultrasound to induce regional hyperthermia monitored with thermistor, which is inserted through co-axial needle. Tumour has final biopsy taken and third ('released') blood test is taken. General Anaesthetic is reversed and Participant goes to Recovery.

epochs.name: FOLLOW-UP
epochs.label: Recovery & Follow-up
epochs.description: End of intervention, start of recovery. Participants will be in the study for less than three months following entry on study and the participant will be followed up clinically for a maximum of 37 days and radiologically for a maximum of 60 days after intervention (Day 1). Following the completion of the study, patients will receive standard care until disease progression or death.

epochs.description: Patient identified as possibly eligible for TARDOX study (via MDT, Oncology clinic, direct referral to trials unit, PIC sites or other appropriate referral route)

estimands.name: EST_PR_01
estimands.description: Estimand corresponding to the primary objective evaluating the feasibility of targeted doxorubicin release using ThermoDox® and FUS in cancer patients
estimands.populationSummary: Evaluable patients with liver tumors receiving the intervention

estimands.populationSummary: Evaluable patients with liver tumors receiving the intervention

studyDesigns.rationale: To date, purely pharmacological approaches have failed to address what is essentially a three-fold challenge: (i) to deliver therapeutically significant concentrations of active agents to the tumour vasculature while minimizing off-target effects; (ii) to release the therapeutic agent 'on-demand' at the target site; and, (iii) to improve the distribution and spread of the therapeutic agent against the intra-tumoural pressure gradient in order to achieve a therapeutically relevant concentration throughout the tumour.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: To determine whether targeted release of doxorubicin from ThermoDox® using mild hyperthermia generated non-invasively by focused ultrasound (FUS) is feasible in cancer patients

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01
endpoints.text: A demonstrable two-fold increase in, or value exceeding 10μg/g of, the concentration of intra-tumoural doxorubicin at the treated tumour site following FUS-induced mild hyperthermia
endpoints.description: A demonstrable two-fold increase in*, or value exceeding 10μg/g of, the concentration of intra-tumoural doxorubicin at the treated tumour site following FUS-induced mild hyperthermia § Quantification of intra-tumoural drug release will be achieved by direct analysis of the intra-tumoural biopsy samples using standardised curve(s)

objectives.description: To determine whether targeted release of doxorubicin from ThermoDox® ('drug') using mild hyperthermia generated non-invasively by focused ultrasound (FUS) is feasible in cancer patients

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_01
objectives.text: To determine optimal FUS exposure parameters for a range of participant Body Mass Indices (BMIs) and tumour locations within the liver

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_01
endpoints.text: Achievement of the desired range of mild hyperthermia in the target tissue as monitored by the implanted thermometry device during FUS exposure
endpoints.description: Achievement of the desired range of mild hyperthermia in the target tissue as monitored by the implanted thermometry device during FUS exposure

objectives.description: Part I only: To determine optimal FUS exposure parameters for a range of participant Body Mass Indices (BMIs) and tumour locations within the liver

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_02
objectives.text: To assess the safety of FUS-induced mild hyperthermia for drug delivery

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_02A
endpoints.text: Persistence of cell viability stain or percentage of cell or tissue necrosis under 30% following FUS exposure
endpoints.description: Persistence of cell viability stain or percentage of cell or tissue necrosis under 30% following FUS exposure, as assessed by cytological or histological methods 16 ‡ ,§

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_02B
endpoints.text: Adverse events deemed related to FUS up to 30 days post-intervention
endpoints.description: Adverse events deemed related to FUS up to 30 days post-intervention

objectives.description: To assess the safety of FUS-induced mild hyperthermia for drug delivery

objectives.name: OBJ_SEC_03
objectives.text: To assess the local and systemic cytotoxic effects of ThermoDox® in this setting

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_03A
endpoints.text: Significant bone marrow suppression, deranged liver function and liver toxicity
endpoints.description: Significant bone marrow suppression, deranged liver function and liver toxicity

endpoints.name: EP_SEC_03B
endpoints.text: Adverse events deemed related to ThermoDox® up to 30 days post-intervention
endpoints.description: Adverse events deemed related to ThermoDox® up to 30 days post-intervention

objectives.description: To assess the local and systemic cytotoxic effects of ThermoDox® in this setting

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_01
objectives.text: To establish the validity of other minimally-invasive and non-invasive methods of evaluating intratumoural uptake of doxorubicin as an alternative to that used in the primary endpoint

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_01A
endpoints.text: Fluorescence microscopy of biopsy samples showing at least twice the fluorescence levels of control
endpoints.description: Released biopsy samples exhibiting regions of nuclear or background fluorescence levels at least twice that of a suitable control, as assessed by fluorescence microscopy ‡ ,§

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_01B
endpoints.text: MR-Spectroscopy showing statistically significant increase in tumour:background signal intensity ratio
endpoints.description: Liver MRI/MR-Spectroscopy indicating targeted delivery of doxorubicin to the tumour by demonstrating a statistically significant increase in the tumour:background ratio of signal intensity following intervention compared to baseline

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_01C
endpoints.text: Statistically significant decrease in total doxorubicin plasma levels following HIFU-induced release
endpoints.description: A statistically significant decrease in total doxorubicin plasma levels over time-matched clearance levels, following HIFU-induced release

objectives.description: To establish the validity of other minimally-invasive and non-invasive methods of evaluating intratumoural uptake of doxorubicin as an alternative to that used in the primary endpoint

objectives.name: OBJ_EXP_02
objectives.text: To determine whether the dose of doxorubicin released has a therapeutically significant effect on the target tumour

endpoints.name: EP_EXP_02
endpoints.text: Radiological evidence of tumour response assessed by CHOI, RECIST, and SUV max
endpoints.description: Radiological evidence of tumour response at timepoints over 30 up to 60 days in the FUS-targeted tumour and control(s), as assessed by principles of CHOI and RECIST response evaluation using MRI and CT [11, 12] and SUV max using PET-CT [13] §

objectives.description: To determine whether the dose of doxorubicin released has a therapeutically significant effect on the target tumour

studyDesigns.description: Phase 1 prospective non-randomised open label single site study to determine whether targeted release of doxorubicin from ThermoDox® ('drug') using mild hyperthermia generated non-invasively by FUS is possible in cancer patients. All participants will receive ThermoDox® and FUS. The study will run in two parts. Part I will identify optimal FUS exposure parameters...

scheduleTimelines.name: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: Main timeline for the study design.

footnotes.text: The ultrasound screening may optionally be performed in advance of screening for the main study, or during the study screening.
Study consent is usually taken during the screening visit but occasionally in advance (e.g. in clinic), but must occur before study procedures are performed outside of standard care.

Best efforts will be made to ensure all screening procedures & required tests occur during a single screening visit but, according to resources and patient preference, one or more additional screening visit(s) may occasionally be required.
It is not compulsory for participants to be admitted on day -1. I.e. the patient can attend to complete pre-intervention assessments and return on day 1 to be admitted for the intervention.
Echocardiogram will only be repeated if clinically indicated.

The 4 week follow-up visit will mark the end of the study and the participant will be free to consider other studies from this point, however scans may be performed after this time point in the flexible scan follow-up visit if that participant is not already enrolled on another study at that time.

The flexible radiology follow-up visit will be chosen based on patient preference, available imaging slots and emerging radiological data. Patient visits will be minimised where possible. The flexible scan visit will be performed at least a week either side the 4 week follow-up visit, and may coincide with the 2 week follow-up visit.
Full Blood Count (FBC); including Haemoglobin (Hb), white blood cells (WBC) with differential count (neutrophils and lymphocytes) and platelets.
Biochemical profile to include U&Es, (Na+, K+, Ur, Cr), phosphate, calcium, LFTs (total protein, ALP, Albumin, ALT or AST, Bili, LDH) (validity period for bloods is 3 days).
Group & Save, as standard for surgical candidates, date of valid test to be recorded on CRF, not blood group. Can alternatively be performed on d-1.
Coagulation studies are performed at baseline only (PT & APTT).
During the Ultrasound Session feasibility of intervention is confirmed and a target tumour will be selected by ultrasound. Only one screening ultrasound is required.
MRI includes the abdomen. Measurements made using the modified RECIST 1.1 criteria.

Liver MRI includes the abdomen. The first Liver MRI/MR Spectroscopy following intervention can alternatively be performed on the same day as intervention, if intervention is in the morning. It should occur within 36 hours of the completion of intervention, and ideally within 24 hours where possible.
Liver pCT includes the abdomen. Measurements made using CHOI and the modified RECIST 1.1 criteria.
Three blood samples for pharmacokinetics in both Phases I and II (Pre-Treatment, Unreleased drug, Released drug).
Three biopsies in Part I (Pre-Treatment, Unreleased drug, Released drug), two biopsies in Part II (Released drug).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Pathologically confirmed advanced solid tumour with liver metastasis suitable for intervention (as assessed by ultrasound or other radiological methods). In addition confirmed primary liver tumours
eligibilityCriteria.text: Pathologically confirmed advanced solid tumour with liver metastasis suitable for intervention (as assessed by ultrasound or other radiological methods). In addition confirmed primary liver tumours (hepatocellular carcinoma or cholangiocarcinoma) can be included.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Will have progressed or remained stable on conventional chemotherapy.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Will have progressed or remained stable on conventional chemotherapy.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have serious illnesses including, but not limited to, congestive heart failure (NYHA class III or IV functional classification); life threatening cardiac arrhythmia; or myocardial infarction or cerebral vascular accident within the last 6 months.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have serious illnesses including, but not limited to, congestive heart failure (NYHA class III or IV functional classification); life threatening cardiac arrhythmia; or myocardial infarction or cerebral vascular accident within the last 6 months.

eligibilityCriteria.text: Have on going significant infection (chest, urine, blood, intra-abdominal).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have uncontrolled diabetes.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have uncontrolled diabetes.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Pregnant or breast-feeding. In women of childbearing potential, a negative pregnancy test (serum) is required within 30 days prior to study intervention.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Pregnant or breast-feeding. In women of childbearing potential, a negative pregnancy test (serum) is required within 30 days prior to study intervention.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Female participants of child bearing potential and male participants whose partner is of child bearing potential who are not willing to practice an acceptable form of contraception (i.e. oral contraceptive, diaphragm, cervical cap, condom, surgical sterility) during the study and for 6 months thereafter. Women whose partner has or men who have undergone a vasectomy must use a second form of birth control.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Female participants of child bearing potential and male participants whose partner is of child bearing potential who are not willing to practice an acceptable form of contraception (i.e. oral contraceptive, diaphragm, cervical cap, condom, surgical sterility) during the study and for 6 months thereafter. Women whose partner has or men who have undergone a vasectomy must use a second form of birth control.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have any known allergic reactions to any of the drugs or liposomal components or intravenous imaging agents to be used in this study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have any known allergic reactions to any of the drugs or liposomal components or intravenous imaging agents to be used in this study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have portal or hepatic vein tumour invasion/thrombosis.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have portal or hepatic vein tumour invasion/thrombosis.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Inadequate Haematological and Biochemical indices:

| Lab Test | Exclude if: |
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Inadequate Haematological and Biochemical indices:

| Lab Test | Exclude if: |
|-------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| INR | > 1.5 times the institution's upper normal limit (UNL)* |
| Absolute neutrophil count | <1,500/mm 3 (or < 1.5 (10^9/L)) |
| Platelet count | < 60,000 /mm 3 (or <60 (10^9/L)) |
| Hgb | < 9.0 g/dL** |
| Serum creatinine, or Calculated creatinine clearance (CrCl) | ≥ 2.5 mg/dL (or ≥ 221 µmol/L) ≤ 25.0 mL/min. |
| Serum bilirubin | |
| | > 3.0 mg/dL (or >51 µmol/L ) |
| Serum albumin | < 2.8 g/dL (or <28 g/L) |

* Except in patients who are therapeutically anticoagulated for medical conditions unrelated to cancer such as atrial fibrillation. Subjects may be re-screened after the condition is treated or anticoagulant is withheld

** Unless the haemoglobin value has been stable, the patient is cardiovascularly stable, asymptomatic, and judged to withstand the FUS procedure

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have contraindications to receiving doxorubicin including prior sensitivity (rash, dyspnoea, wheezing, urticarial or other symptoms) attributed to anthracyclines or other liposomal drugs.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have contraindications to receiving doxorubicin including prior sensitivity (rash, dyspnoea, wheezing, urticarial or other symptoms) attributed to anthracyclines or other liposomal drugs.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Use of chemotherapy or of an investigational drug within 30 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is longer, preceding the intervention.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Use of chemotherapy or of an investigational drug within 30 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is longer, preceding the intervention.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have medically significant active infection.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have medically significant active infection.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have Child-Pugh Class C liver disease, or Class A-B with encephalopathy and/or refractory ascites (Appendix 3).
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have Child-Pugh Class C liver disease, or Class A-B with encephalopathy and/or refractory ascites (Appendix 3).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented HIV positive.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented HIV positive.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented diagnosis of haemochromatosis.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented diagnosis of haemochromatosis.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented history of contrast-induced nephropathy.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented history of contrast-induced nephropathy.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Have any of the following contraindications for liver biopsy:
- a. Suspected liver haemangioma or other vascular tumour
- b. Tense ascites
- c. Known cystic liver disease*
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Have any of the following contraindications for liver biopsy:
- a. Suspected liver haemangioma or other vascular tumour
- b. Tense ascites
- c. Known cystic liver disease*
- d. Extra-hepatic biliary obstruction*

(* Relative contraindications only and may be non-exclusive at discretion of the study team)

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other medical or psychiatric conditions or laboratory abnormalities that the investigator considers would make the patient a poor trial candidate.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Other medical or psychiatric conditions or laboratory abnormalities that the investigator considers would make the patient a poor trial candidate.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Have received a life-time dose of doxorubicin > 450 mg/m 2 or a life-time dose of epirubicin > 900 mg/m 2 or any dose of both.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Have received a life-time dose of doxorubicin > 450 mg/m 2 or a life-time dose of epirubicin > 900 mg/m 2 or any dose of both.

studyInterventions.name: ThermoDox®
studyInterventions.description: Investigational medicinal product administered intravenously to achieve targeted release of doxorubicin in liver tumors using mild hyperthermia induced by FUS.

administrations.name: ThermoDox® IV infusion

duration.text: Single dose on day of intervention

administrations.description: Infused in 250ml 5% dextrose immediately before final FUS exposure. All FUS exposures after infusion start completed within 4 hours.

studyInterventions.name: Dexamethasone premedication
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication to prevent hypersensitivity reactions to liposomes in ThermoDox®.

administrations.name: Oral dexamethasone

duration.text: 3 doses over 24 hours prior to intervention

administrations.description: Every 12 hours for 3 doses starting 24 hours prior to ThermoDox® treatment; third dose morning of treatment.

administrations.name: IV dexamethasone

duration.text: Single dose on day of intervention

administrations.description: Administered 30-60 minutes prior to ThermoDox® infusion.

studyInterventions.name: Chlorpheniramine premedication
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication to prevent hypersensitivity reactions.

administrations.name: IV chlorpheniramine

duration.text: Single dose on day of intervention

administrations.description: Administered 30-60 minutes prior to ThermoDox® infusion.

administrations.name: IV ranitidine

duration.text: Single dose on day of intervention

administrations.description: Administered 30-60 minutes prior to ThermoDox® infusion.
